knot_assessments.notes: Square knot technique with good initial throw
Proper reverse direction throw with hand alternation
Additional securing throw completes secure knot

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Good tissue approximation with complete closure

technique_summary: Well-executed intracorporeal knot tying with proper square knot technique and hand alternation. Teachers disagreed on total time (30s vs 65s) and knot type (square vs surgeon's knot first throw). Consensus favors intermediate timing and standard square knot technique based on typical intracorporeal knot demonstrations.
improvement_suggestions: Maintain consistent tension throughout knot tying process
Consider slightly faster instrument manipulation for improved efficiency
strengths: Proper square knot technique with appropriate hand alternation
Good needle handling and instrument control
Secure final knot with complete tissue approximation
